title: Nephrodium hirtum
taxonomy: Nephrodium hirtum belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as ferns. Within this division, it is classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida. This class is further divided into the order Polypodiales, and Nephrodium hirtum is a part of this order. The order Polypodiales is characterized by the presence of a distinct sori arrangement and the lack of a distinct rhizome. Nephrodium hirtum is specifically a species of fern that is native to tropical regions of the world.
uses: Nephrodium hirtum, a species of fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against infections and promote tissue repair. In some traditional medicine practices, Nephrodium hirtum is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Nephrodium hirtum for medicinal purposes.
